name: consul
explanation: Consul's HTTP API listens on a single well-known port (8500 by default for the agent, 8501 for HTTPS) and the check just needs a base URL — it discovers everything else (`/v1/status/leader`, `/v1/agent/self`, `/v1/agent/metrics?format=prometheus`) from there. The shipped auto_conf templates `http://%%host%%:8500`, which is exactly the canonical default. ACL tokens are optional and only required if the operator has enabled ACLs (default deny is becoming common, but there is still a wide deployment base running unauthenticated localhost agents). Probing port 8500 for `/v1/status/leader` returning a JSON-quoted IP:port string is a strong, integration-agnostic HTTP probe.
notes: Consul deployments with `acl.default_policy = deny` need an ACL token to read most endpoints — flag that the auth-optional caveat applies (similar to elastic/mongo). The unauthenticated `/v1/status/leader` and Prometheus endpoint still respond, so a probe can still fingerprint Consul; metric collection breadth is what suffers.
